(Sarah of the Clue Crew gives the clue from Green Gate in Gdansk, Poland.) Originally designed to house royalty, Zielona Brama, also known as Green Gate, once served as offices for Lech Walesa, who formed this first Polish independent trade union

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sarah of the Clue Crew gives the clue from Gdansk, Poland.) Legend says this Roman god, a symbol of Gdansk's ties to the sea, was angered by coins in his fountain, so he lashed out, & shards from the coins are now part of the local beverage Goldwasser

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sarah of the Clue Crew gives the clue from a street in Gdansk, Poland.) Hitler demanded the cession of Gdansk & its shipyards, but it was a free city due to this treaty named for a French locale; Poland refused, & the Nazis attacked, precipitating World War II

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sarah of the Clue Crew gives the clue from a shop in Gdansk, Poland.) Found in 40-million-year-old Baltic sands, this Baltic gold is used to make treasures for which Gdansk is famous
